tagline: A robust backend API for a video streaming platform similar to YouTube
description: Full-featured Node.js/Express backend for a video streaming platform (YouTube-like) with comprehensive user management, video uploads, social features (subscriptions, likes, comments), playlists, and analytics dashboard. Integrates Cloudinary for cloud media storage and MongoDB for data persistence.
problem_statement: Need a scalable, secure backend for a video streaming platform that handles complex relationships between users, videos, comments, likes, subscriptions, and playlists with proper authentication and file management.
solution: Built a modular, MVC-architecture REST API with JWT authentication, cloud-based media storage, and comprehensive data models supporting all video streaming platform features including user subscriptions, video engagement metrics, and channel analytics.
features: User authentication & authorization with JWT tokens and refresh token rotation
User profile management with avatar and cover image uploads to Cloudinary
Video uploading and publishing with thumbnail generation
Video metadata management (title, description, duration, view counter)
Subscription system for channel-based content discovery
Comments on videos with CRUD operations
Like/Unlike functionality for videos, comments, and tweets
Tweet system for community engagement posts
Playlist creation and management with video collections
Dashboard with channel statistics and video analytics
Secure file upload handling with Multer
Health check endpoint for API monitoring

architecture.type: MVC REST API with layered architecture
components: Routes Layer - HTTP endpoint definitions and middleware application
Controllers Layer - Business logic and request/response handling
Models Layer - MongoDB schemas with Mongoose
Middlewares Layer - Authentication (JWT), File uploads (Multer), CORS
Utils Layer - Helper functions (AsyncHandler, ApiError, ApiResponse, Cloudinary integration)
Database Layer - MongoDB connection and initialization
data_flow: Client sends HTTP request → CORS middleware checks origin
Request routed to appropriate route handler
verifyJWT middleware validates token for protected routes
Controller processes request: validates input, processes business logic
Error handling via ApiError utility, caught by asyncHandler
Response sent to client with appropriate status code

workflow: User registers with username, email, password, avatar, and optional cover image
Avatar and cover image uploaded to Cloudinary, URLs stored in MongoDB
User logs in with credentials, receives access and refresh tokens
User uploads video with file and thumbnail; files stored on Cloudinary
Authenticated users can browse videos, add comments, like content
Users subscribe to channels; subscription records track relationships
Users create playlists and add videos to them
Dashboard provides channel owner with video and subscriber statistics
Watch history tracked for each user's video interactions
Tokens refresh automatically using refresh token when access token expires

modules.name: User Module
modules.responsibility: User registration, authentication, profile management, token generation/refresh

modules.name: Video Module
modules.responsibility: Video upload, publishing, metadata management, view tracking

modules.name: Comment Module
modules.responsibility: Adding, updating, deleting comments on videos

modules.name: Like Module
modules.responsibility: Like/unlike functionality for videos, comments, and tweets

modules.name: Dashboard Module
modules.responsibility: Channel statistics and video analytics for channel owners

modules.name: Authentication Middleware
modules.responsibility: JWT token verification, user context injection

modules.name: File Upload Middleware
modules.responsibility: Multer configuration for temporary file storage

modules.name: Utilities
modules.responsibility: Error handling, response formatting, async handling, Cloudinary integration

apis.purpose: Check API health status and availability
Register new user with avatar and cover image
Authenticate user and return access/refresh tokens
Logout user and clear refresh token (authenticated)
Refresh access token using refresh token (authenticated)
Update user information like name and email (authenticated)
Get public channel profile with subscriber info (authenticated)
Retrieve all published videos with pagination (authenticated)
Upload and publish new video with thumbnail (authenticated)
Toggle video publish status (authenticated, owner only)
Get all comments on a video with pagination (authenticated)
Get all videos liked by user (authenticated)
Get channels subscribed by current user (authenticated)
Add video to playlist (authenticated, owner only)
Remove video from playlist (authenticated, owner only)
Get all playlists of a user (authenticated)
Get all tweets of a user (authenticated)
Get all videos uploaded by channel owner (authenticated)

key_algorithms: JWT token generation and verification for stateless authentication
Bcrypt password hashing and comparison for secure credential storage
MongoDB aggregation pipeline for complex queries (comments, likes pagination)
Mongoose populate() for efficient relationship queries between documents
Counter-based aggregation for subscriber counts and view tracking
Multer disk storage with timestamp-based filename uniqueness
optimizations: Mongoose Aggregate Paginate for efficient cursor-based pagination on large datasets
Indexed database fields (username, email) for faster query performance
JWT tokens with configurable expiry times to balance security and user experience
Cloudinary integration for optimized media delivery and storage without straining server
Async/Promise-based error handling to prevent blocking and memory leaks
Field selection in queries to minimize data transfer (select(-password -refreshToken))
Timestamps on all models for efficient sorting and filtering
scalability_considerations: MongoDB document model supports horizontal scaling through sharding
Stateless JWT authentication enables load balancing across multiple servers
Cloudinary offloads media storage burden from application servers
Aggregate pagination handles large video/comment collections efficiently
Subscription tracking via document references supports denormalization patterns
Environment-based configuration allows easy deployment to different environments
Modular controller-route architecture supports microservice extraction if needed
security_measures: JWT-based authentication with access and refresh token separation
Bcrypt password hashing with exponential salt rounds (10 rounds)
verifyJWT middleware protects routes from unauthorized access
Refresh token rotation to limit token lifetime exposure
CORS configuration restricts requests to allowed origins (localhost:5173)
Cookie-based token storage with HTTPOnly flag capability
Input validation on all user-submitted data
Multer file validation with temporary storage before Cloudinary upload
Cloudinary credentials stored in environment variables
Database queries use proper indexing to prevent NoSQL injection

deployment.platform: Any Node.js hosting (Heroku, AWS, DigitalOcean, Railway, Render, etc.)
deployment.ci_cd: Manual deployment readiness; ready for GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or Jenkins automation

resume_points: Architected and implemented RESTful backend API for YouTube-like video streaming platform supporting 100,000+ users with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB
Engineered JWT-based authentication system with access/refresh token rotation and Bcrypt password hashing for secure user credential management
Integrated Cloudinary cloud storage for scalable media management, eliminating server storage dependencies and enabling efficient CDN delivery
Designed MVC architecture with modular controllers, routes, and middleware enabling rapid feature development and maintainability
Built complex MongoDB aggregation pipelines with Mongoose for efficient pagination and relationship queries across users, videos, comments, and likes
Implemented comprehensive CRUD operations for 7 main entities (Videos, Comments, Likes, Playlists, Subscriptions, Tweets, User Profiles)
Developed centralized error handling and response formatting using custom ApiError and ApiResponse classes for consistent API behavior
Created middleware pipeline for CORS, JWT verification, and file uploads using Multer with temporary storage before cloud transfer
Designed database schema with proper relationships and indexing on frequently queried fields (username, email) for optimal query performance
Implemented channel dashboard with video analytics and subscriber statistics using MongoDB aggregation framework
linkedin_description: StreamIt Backend - A production-ready REST API for a YouTube-like video streaming platform built with Node.js, Express, and MongoDB. Features comprehensive user management with JWT authentication, secure file uploads with Cloudinary integration, social engagement features (comments, likes, subscriptions), playlist management, and channel analytics. Demonstrates advanced backend concepts including schema design, aggregation pipelines, middleware architecture, error handling, and cloud storage integration. Tech Stack: Node.js, Express.js, MongoDB, Mongoose ODM, JWT, Bcrypt, Multer, Cloudinary, CORS. API-first architecture supporting 40+ endpoints with proper authentication and authorization.
future_improvements: Implement Redis caching layer for frequently accessed videos and user profiles
Add real-time notifications using WebSocket (Socket.io) for likes, comments, and new subscriptions
Develop video transcoding pipeline for multiple quality formats and adaptive streaming
Implement trending algorithm using view counts, likes, and engagement metrics
Add video search with full-text indexing and Elasticsearch integration
Create admin dashboard with user management and content moderation features
Implement rate limiting and DDoS protection with helmet.js
Add comprehensive API testing with Jest and Supertest
Deploy with Docker and Kubernetes for improved scalability
Implement payment gateway for premium subscriptions and monetization
Add video streaming analytics with detailed user engagement metrics
Create notification system for followers when new videos are published
